behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is a local backoff-calculation rule (attempt count/base delay/max cap), not a cross-module handoff failure. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none apply. Rejection-gate: higher levels are disqualified by the behavior text focusing only on delay math ("doubles... up to a configured maximum"), which can fail entirely inside one pure function.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit test of the backoff function: verify exponential growth across consecutive failures and capping at configured max, with boundary cases (0 failures, first failure, just before cap, at/after cap).

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time." Slot B (decisive): handoff between response-classification and retry-policy modules. Slot C (miss if lowered): a unit test of either classifier or backoff alone can miss a wiring bug where 429/headers are parsed but not passed correctly to delay computation. Integration-brake checks: no real OS/network/subprocess required for the failure itself; no AI output dependency; no multi-agentic chain. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because synthetic in-process HTTP response objects can reproduce this failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Wire retry orchestrator + rate-limit classifier + backoff policy with a stub transport returning a rate-limit response (e.g., 429 with retry metadata). Assert that classification triggers retry path and computed delay matches policy input derived from that response.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result..." Slot B (decisive): handoff across retry scheduler, timer control, and request execution path. Slot C (miss if lowered): unit tests may pass on retry decision logic while missing that mutated request options/body are resent incorrectly or not resent at all. Integration-brake checks: no requirement for real network/OS timing primitives; fake clock + stub transport is sufficient. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because behavior does not require real HTTP stack behavior, only correct in-process resend orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with fake clock and stub transport: first response is rate-limited, second is success. Assert original request payload/headers are reused on resend and caller receives only final success value (transparent retry).

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "After the configured maximum number of retries... returns a typed QuotaExhaustedError rather than a generic error." Slot B (decisive): handoff from retry-attempt accounting into error-mapping/typing boundary. Slot C (miss if lowered): a unit test of error class alone misses failures where retry loop throws underlying transport error instead of mapped typed error at exhaustion boundary. Integration-brake checks: no real infra needed; no AI dependency; no multi-step agentic pipeline. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because repeated stubbed rate-limit responses fully exercise the failure path.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with stub transport always returning rate-limit failures; run through max retries and assert rejection is specifically `QuotaExhaustedError` (type and key fields), not raw/generic error.

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "full retry chain... backoff retries, quota tracking... does not loop indefinitely." Slot B (decisive): orchestration/state handoff across multiple local modules (retry controller + quota tracker + termination condition). Slot C (miss if lowered): unit tests of termination predicate can miss integration bug where attempt/quota state is never advanced, causing infinite loop. Integration-brake checks: no real OS/network/subprocess required; no model output dependency; not agentic. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because fake clock and stub transport can deterministically expose non-termination in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with fake clock, bounded watchdog, and stubbed repeated quota failures. Assert attempts stop at configured terminal condition, promise settles with expected exhaustion error, and no extra retries are scheduled afterward.

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket." Slot B (decisive): shared-state handoff/concurrency boundary across caller-facing API and quota manager. Slot C (miss if lowered): unit tests per-call path can pass while missing race bugs where callers accidentally maintain separate counters/buckets. Integration-brake checks: no real network/OS primitive required beyond in-process async scheduling; no AI dependency; no multi-agentic chain. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified by explicit scope "same process," indicating local module wiring and concurrency control.
behaviors.test_description: Integration concurrency test: start multiple `call()` invocations simultaneously against a stub transport, assert global quota accounting reflects all callers collectively (not per-caller), and that throttling/exhaustion decisions are based on one shared bucket.

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "multiple requests are waiting... dispatched in the order... submitted." Slot B (decisive): queueing/dispatcher handoff across async submission and send scheduler. Slot C (miss if lowered): unit-testing queue data structure alone can miss real orchestration bug where scheduler dequeues out of order under async wakeups. Integration-brake checks: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement; no AI dependency; no multi-agentic chain. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because ordering failures can be reproduced with in-process async tasks and stubbed sender.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with controlled async timing: enqueue several calls quickly while quota blocks immediate send, then release capacity and assert send order and completion order match submission order (FIFO dispatch).
